import os

os.environ["KMP_DUPLICATE_LIB_OK"] = "True"

import sys
sys.path.append("/Users/niazahmad/Desktop/Research/explainable-video-anomaly-detection/models/aed-mae")


import torch
import cv2 
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from model.model_factory import mae_cvt_patch8

print(f"{'='*70}")

In [67]:
print("\n[STEP 1/5] Loading Self-Distilled MAE model...")
print("This will take 30-60 seconds...")

teacher_ckpt = "/Users/niazahmad/Desktop/Research/explainable-video-anomaly-detection/models/aed-mae/experiments/shanghai/checkpoint-best.pth"
student_ckpt = "/Users/niazahmad/Desktop/Research/explainable-video-anomaly-detection/models/aed-mae/experiments/shanghai/checkpoint-best-student.pth"

model = mae_cvt_patch8(
    norm_pix_loss=False,
    img_size=(160, 320),
    use_only_masked_tokens_ab=False,
    abnormal_score_func=["l1", "L1"],
    masking_method="random_masking",
    grad_weighted_loss=True
).float()

print("✅ Model architecture created")
print(f"   - Input size: 160×320")
print(f"   - Patch size: 8×8")
print(f"   - Grid: 20×40 patches")
print(f"   - Total patches: {20*40} = 800")

teacher = torch.load(teacher_ckpt, map_location='cpu', weights_only=False)["model"]
student = torch.load(student_ckpt, map_location='cpu', weights_only=False)["model"]


for key in student.keys():
    if "student" in key:
        teacher[key] = student[key]

model.load_state_dict(teacher, strict=False)
model.eval()

model.train_TS = True
model.abnormal_score_func = "L1"

print("✅ Model weights loaded")
print(f"   - Teacher checkpoint: {os.path.getsize(teacher_ckpt) / 1e6:.1f} MB")
print(f"   - Student checkpoint: {os.path.getsize(student_ckpt) / 1e6:.1f} MB")
print(f"   - Mode: Student-Teacher (train_TS=True)")
print(f"   - Score function: L1")

# VERIFICATION: Check model has key components
print("\n🔍 VERIFICATION: Model has required components?")
print(f"   - Has 'abnormal_score_TS' method? {hasattr(model, 'abnormal_score_TS')}")
print(f"   - Has 'unpatchify' method? {hasattr(model, 'unpatchify')}")
print(f"   - train_TS enabled? {model.train_TS}")

print("\n✅ STEP 1 COMPLETE - Model ready for inference")

In [70]:
print("\n[STEP 3/5] Preprocessing frames...")
print("="*70)


frame_normal_idx = 50
frame_anomaly_idx = 190


import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as patches

video_dir = "/Users/niazahmad/Desktop/Research/explainable-video-anomaly-detection/SHANGHAI/SHANGHAI_Test/frames/01_0025"


def load_and_prcoess_frame(frame_idx, video_dir):
    prev_idx = frame_idx - 3
    next_idx = frame_idx + 3

    prev_frame = cv2.imread(os.path.join(video_dir, f"{prev_idx:03d}.jpg"))
    frame = cv2.imread(os.path.join(video_dir, f"{frame_idx:03d}.jpg"))
    next_frame = cv2.imread(os.path.join(video_dir, f"{next_idx:03d}.jpg"))
    

    print(f"   - Loaded frames: {prev_idx}, {frame_idx}, {next_idx}")
    print(f"   - Original frame size: {frame.shape} Height x Width x Channels")
    print(f"   - Data type: {frame.dtype}")
    print(f"   - Value range: {frame.min()} to {frame.max()}")

    # Step 1 Resize to 160x320
    prev_frame = cv2.resize(prev_frame, (320, 160))
    frame = cv2.resize(frame, (320, 160))
    next_frame = cv2.resize(next_frame, (320, 160))

    # Step 2 Convert BGR to RGB as opencv loads in BGR format
    prev_frame_rgb = cv2.cvtColor(prev_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
    frame_rgb = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
    next_frame_rgb = cv2.cvtColor(next_frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)

    # Step 3 Normalize to -1 to 1
    prev_frame = (prev_frame_rgb.astype(np.float32) - 127.5) / 127.5
    frame = (frame_rgb.astype(np.float32) - 127.5) / 127.5
    next_frame = (next_frame_rgb.astype(np.float32) - 127.5) / 127.5

    motion_grad = np.abs(prev_frame - next_frame)
    motion_grad_avg = np.mean(motion_grad, axis=2, keepdims=True).astype(np.float32)

    # Convert to pytorch tensor and unsqueeze to 4d
    prev_frame = torch.from_numpy(prev_frame).unsqueeze(0)
    frame = torch.from_numpy(frame).unsqueeze(0)
    next_frame = torch.from_numpy(next_frame).unsqueeze(0)
    motion_grad_avg = torch.from_numpy(motion_grad_avg).unsqueeze(0)

    print(f"   - Prev frame shape: {prev_frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Frame shape: {frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Next frame shape: {next_frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Motion grad avg shape: {motion_grad_avg.shape}")

    prev_frame = prev_frame.permute(0, 3, 1, 2)
    frame = frame.permute(0, 3, 1, 2)
    next_frame = next_frame.permute(0, 3, 1, 2)
    motion_grad_avg = motion_grad_avg.permute(0, 3, 1, 2)

    print(f"   - Prev frame shape: {prev_frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Frame shape: {frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Next frame shape: {next_frame.shape}")
    print(f"   - Motion grad avg shape: {motion_grad_avg.shape}")

    # combine three frames to a single tensor

    frames = torch.cat((prev_frame, frame, next_frame), dim=1)
  

    return {
        'frames': frames,
        'motion_grad_avg': motion_grad_avg,
        'curr_rgb': frame_rgb,
        'motion_grad': motion_grad_avg,
        'raw_frame': frame,
    }
    
    
print("-"*70)
print("Processing normal frame")
data_normal = load_and_prcoess_frame(frame_normal_idx, video_dir)

print("-"*70)
print("Processing anomaly frame")
data_anomaly = load_and_prcoess_frame(frame_anomaly_idx, video_dir)

print("-"*70)
print("process complete and ready for inference")

Step 4 Running inference on the images¶

In [97]:
print("-"*70)
print("Step 4/5: Running inference on the images")

import json
import time


def _as_float(value):
    try:
        return float(value)
    except Exception:
        return None


def _log_event(hypothesis_id, location, message, data):
    payload = {
        "sessionId": "debug-session",
        "runId": "run1",
        "hypothesisId": hypothesis_id,
        "location": location,
        "message": message,
        "data": data,
        "timestamp": int(time.time() * 1000),
    }
    with open("/Users/niazahmad/Desktop/Research/cursor/debug.log", "a") as log_file:
        log_file.write(json.dumps(payload) + "\n")


def run_inference_v2(data, frame_idx, label):
    with torch.no_grad():
        model.eval()
        
        # Get frame-level scores using full model call
        frame = data['frames'][:, 3:6, :, :]
        zeror_anamoly_target = torch.zeros(1, 1, 160, 320)
        target = torch.cat([frame, zeror_anamoly_target], dim=1)
        
        _, _, _, scores = model(
            data['frames'],
            target,
            data['motion_grad_avg'],
            mask_ratio=0.5,
        )
        
        score_st = scores[0].item()
        score_teacher = scores[1].item()
        score_combined = score_st + score_teacher
        
        # Now get spatial maps using separate calls
        latent, mask, ids_restore = model.forward_encoder(
            data['frames'],
            mask_ratio=0.5,
            grad_mask=data['motion_grad_avg']
        )
        
        pred_stud, pred_teacher = model.forward_decoder_TS(latent, ids_restore)
        
        recon_teacher = model.unpatchify(pred_teacher)
        recon_student = model.unpatchify(pred_stud)
        
        # Teacher-Student difference (spatial anomaly signal)
        teacher_student_diff = torch.abs(recon_teacher - recon_student)
        anomaly_map_diff = teacher_student_diff[:, 3:4, :, :].squeeze().cpu().numpy()
        rgb_diff = teacher_student_diff[:, :3, :, :].mean(dim=1).squeeze().cpu().numpy()
        
        # Denormalize
        anomaly_map_diff_scaled = (anomaly_map_diff * 127.5).clip(0, 255)
        
        # Also get teacher's reconstruction
        recon_rgb = recon_teacher[:, :3, :, :].squeeze(0).permute(1, 2, 0).cpu().numpy()
        recon_rgb = (recon_rgb * 127.5 + 127.5).clip(0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
        
        print(f"Frame {frame_idx} - Score ST: {score_st:.4f}, Score Teacher: {score_teacher:.4f}, Score Combined: {score_combined:.4f}")
        
        return {
            'score_st': score_st,
            'score_teacher': score_teacher,
            'score_combined': score_combined,
            'anomaly_map_diff': anomaly_map_diff_scaled,
            'rgb_diff': rgb_diff,
            'recon_rgb': recon_rgb,
            'pred_teacher': pred_teacher,
            'pred_stud': pred_stud,
        }
            

    









results_normal = run_inference_v2(data_normal, frame_normal_idx, 0)

results_anomaly = run_inference_v2(data_anomaly, frame_anomaly_idx, 1)


print("\n" + "="*70)
print("📊 COMPARISON:")
print("="*70)
print(f"{'Frame':<20} {'Combined Score':<20} {'Expected':<20}")
print("-"*70)
print(f"{frame_normal_idx} (Normal)         {results_normal['score_combined']:<20.4f} LOW (< 0.6)")
print(f"{frame_anomaly_idx} (Anomalous)      {results_anomaly['score_combined']:<20.4f} HIGH (> 0.6)")
print("="*70)

# STEP 5: Visualize Results Side-by-Side
# Freeman: "Build intuition by looking at outputs"

print("\n[STEP 5/5] Generating visualizations...")
print("="*70)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(20, 10))
fig.suptitle('Day 1: Normal vs Anomalous Frame Analysis', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')

# Row 1: NORMAL Frame
axes[0, 0].imshow(data_normal['curr_rgb'])
axes[0, 0].set_title(f'🔵 NORMAL Frame {frame_normal_idx}\nPerson Walking', fontweight='bold')
axes[0, 0].axis('off')

axes[0, 1].imshow(results_normal['recon_rgb'])
axes[0, 1].set_title('Teacher Reconstruction')
axes[0, 1].axis('off')

axes[0, 2].imshow(data_normal['motion_grad'].squeeze(), cmap='hot')
axes[0, 2].set_title('Motion Gradient')
axes[0, 2].axis('off')

axes[0, 3].text(0.5, 0.5, 
                f'Combined Score:\n{results_normal["score_combined"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Student-Teacher:\n{results_normal["score_st"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Teacher Error:\n{results_normal["score_teacher"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Status: ✅ NORMAL',
                ha='center', va='center', fontsize=12,
                bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightblue', alpha=0.8))
axes[0, 3].set_title('Anomaly Scores')
axes[0, 3].axis('off')

# Row 2: ANOMALOUS Frame
axes[1, 0].imshow(data_anomaly['curr_rgb'])
axes[1, 0].set_title(f'🔴 ANOMALOUS Frame {frame_anomaly_idx}\nPerson on Bicycle', 
                      fontweight='bold', color='red')
axes[1, 0].axis('off')

axes[1, 1].imshow(results_anomaly['recon_rgb'])
axes[1, 1].set_title('Teacher Reconstruction')
axes[1, 1].axis('off')

axes[1, 2].imshow(data_anomaly['motion_grad'].squeeze(), cmap='hot')
axes[1, 2].set_title('Motion Gradient')
axes[1, 2].axis('off')

score_color = 'lightcoral' if results_anomaly['score_combined'] > results_normal['score_combined'] else 'lightblue'
axes[1, 3].text(0.5, 0.5, 
                f'Combined Score:\n{results_anomaly["score_combined"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Student-Teacher:\n{results_anomaly["score_st"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Teacher Error:\n{results_anomaly["score_teacher"]:.4f}\n\n'
                f'Status: 🚨 ANOMALY',
                ha='center', va='center', fontsize=12,
                bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor=score_color, alpha=0.8))
axes[1, 3].set_title('Anomaly Scores')
axes[1, 3].axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('day1_normal_vs_anomaly.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
print("💾 Saved: day1_normal_vs_anomaly.png")
plt.show()

print("\n✅ STEP 5 COMPLETE")
print("="*70)

print("\n" + "="*70)
print("GENERATING PREDICTED MAP (Exactly as Paper's Figure 5)")
print("="*70)

with torch.no_grad():
    model.eval()
    
    # === For ANOMALY frame ===
    latent, mask, ids_restore = model.forward_encoder(
        data_anomaly['frames'],
        mask_ratio=0.5,
        grad_mask=data_anomaly['motion_grad_avg']
    )
    
    # Get both student and teacher predictions
    pred_stud, pred_teacher = model.forward_decoder_TS(latent, ids_restore)
    
    # Unpatchify to get 4-channel images
    recon_teacher_4ch = model.unpatchify(pred_teacher)  # (1, 4, 160, 320)
    recon_student_4ch = model.unpatchify(pred_stud)     # (1, 4, 160, 320)
    
    # Extract the 4th channel (anomaly map) - this is "Predicted map" in Figure 1
    teacher_predicted_map = recon_teacher_4ch[:, 3:4, :, :].squeeze().cpu().numpy()
    student_predicted_map = recon_student_4ch[:, 3:4, :, :].squeeze().cpu().numpy()
    
    # Extract RGB reconstructions
    teacher_rgb = recon_teacher_4ch[:, :3, :, :].squeeze().permute(1, 2, 0).cpu().numpy()
    student_rgb = recon_student_4ch[:, :3, :, :].squeeze().permute(1, 2, 0).cpu().numpy()
    
    # Denormalize RGB
    teacher_rgb_denorm = ((teacher_rgb * 127.5) + 127.5).clip(0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
    student_rgb_denorm = ((student_rgb * 127.5) + 127.5).clip(0, 255).astype(np.uint8)
    
    # Denormalize maps: -1 → 0 (normal), +1 → 255 (anomaly)
    teacher_map_denorm = ((teacher_predicted_map * 127.5) + 127.5).clip(0, 255)
    student_map_denorm = ((student_predicted_map * 127.5) + 127.5).clip(0, 255)

print(f"📊 Predicted Map Statistics (Teacher):")
print(f"   Raw range: [{teacher_predicted_map.min():.4f}, {teacher_predicted_map.max():.4f}]")
print(f"   Denormalized range: [{teacher_map_denorm.min():.1f}, {teacher_map_denorm.max():.1f}]")
print(f"   Mean: {teacher_map_denorm.mean():.1f}")

print(f"\n📊 Predicted Map Statistics (Student):")
print(f"   Raw range: [{student_predicted_map.min():.4f}, {student_predicted_map.max():.4f}]")
print(f"   Denormalized range: [{student_map_denorm.min():.1f}, {student_map_denorm.max():.1f}]")
print(f"   Mean: {student_map_denorm.mean():.1f}")

# Create visualization matching Figure 1 layout
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(20, 10))
fig.suptitle(f'Replicating Paper Figure 1 Layout - Frame {frame_anomaly_idx}', 
             fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')

# Row 1: Student
axes[0, 0].imshow(data_anomaly['curr_rgb'])
axes[0, 0].set_title('Input Frame\n(with anomaly)', fontsize=11)
axes[0, 0].axis('off')

axes[0, 1].imshow(student_rgb_denorm)
axes[0, 1].set_title('Student: Predicted Frame', fontsize=11)
axes[0, 1].axis('off')

im1 = axes[0, 2].imshow(student_map_denorm, cmap='gray', vmin=0, vmax=255)
axes[0, 2].set_title(f'Student: Predicted Map\nmean={student_map_denorm.mean():.1f}', fontsize=11)
axes[0, 2].axis('off')
plt.colorbar(im1, ax=axes[0, 2], fraction=0.046)

axes[0, 3].imshow(data_anomaly['curr_rgb'])
axes[0, 3].imshow(student_map_denorm/255, cmap='jet', alpha=0.5)
axes[0, 3].set_title('Student: Overlay', fontsize=11)
axes[0, 3].axis('off')

# Row 2: Teacher
axes[1, 0].imshow(data_anomaly['curr_rgb'])
axes[1, 0].set_title('Input Frame\n(with anomaly)', fontsize=11)
axes[1, 0].axis('off')

axes[1, 1].imshow(teacher_rgb_denorm)
axes[1, 1].set_title('Teacher: Predicted Frame', fontsize=11)
axes[1, 1].axis('off')

im2 = axes[1, 2].imshow(teacher_map_denorm, cmap='gray', vmin=0, vmax=255)
axes[1, 2].set_title(f'Teacher: Predicted Map\nmean={teacher_map_denorm.mean():.1f}', fontsize=11)
axes[1, 2].axis('off')
plt.colorbar(im2, ax=axes[1, 2], fraction=0.046)

axes[1, 3].imshow(data_anomaly['curr_rgb'])
axes[1, 3].imshow(teacher_map_denorm/255, cmap='jet', alpha=0.5)
axes[1, 3].set_title('Teacher: Overlay', fontsize=11)
axes[1, 3].axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('day1_paper_figure1_replication.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# === Check difference between Student and Teacher maps ===
map_difference = np.abs(teacher_map_denorm - student_map_denorm)
print(f"\n📊 Student-Teacher Map Difference:")
print(f"   Max difference: {map_difference.max():.1f}")
print(f"   Mean difference: {map_difference.mean():.1f}")

print("\n" + "="*70)
print("🔍 INTERPRETATION:")
print("   - If both maps are mostly dark (~0): Model predicts 'no anomaly'")
print("   - If Teacher map has bright regions: Model detected anomaly location")
print("   - Figure 5 shows Teacher map with bicycle highlighted white")
print("="*70)

======================================================================
GENERATING PREDICTED MAP (Exactly as Paper's Figure 5)
======================================================================
📊 Predicted Map Statistics (Teacher):
   Raw range: [-1.1483, 0.3063]
   Denormalized range: [0.0, 166.6]
   Mean: 5.3

📊 Predicted Map Statistics (Student):
   Raw range: [-1.4394, -0.2602]
   Denormalized range: [0.0, 94.3]
   Mean: 10.3

📊 Student-Teacher Map Difference:
   Max difference: 156.2
   Mean difference: 10.3

======================================================================
🔍 INTERPRETATION:
   - If both maps are mostly dark (~0): Model predicts 'no anomaly'
   - If Teacher map has bright regions: Model detected anomaly location
   - Figure 5 shows Teacher map with bicycle highlighted white
======================================================================

What the Paper Actually Does:¶

Looking at their code:

During TRAINING (line 296 in forward_loss):

loss = (loss * grad_weights).sum()  # ✅ Uses motion weighting

During INFERENCE (line 362-365 in abnormal_score_TS):

output.append(torch.abs(pred_teacher - pred_stud).mean((2)))  # ❌ NO motion weighting!
return [output[0].mean(1), output[1].mean(1)]  # Just averages all patches

They use motion weighting for training but NOT for inference scoring!

My Idea: Motion-Weighted Anomaly Score¶

Instead of:

score = mean(teacher_student_diff over ALL patches)

I do:

score = weighted_mean(teacher_student_diff, weights=motion_gradient)

This focuses the score on moving regions where anomalies typically occur!

My Implementation:¶

def compute_motion_weighted_score(pred_stud, pred_teacher, motion_grad, patch_size=8):
    """
    Compute teacher-student difference weighted by motion gradient.
    Focuses on moving regions, ignores static background.
    """
    # Compute patch-wise difference
    diff = torch.abs(pred_teacher - pred_stud)  # (1, 800, 256)
    diff_per_patch = diff.mean(dim=2)  # (1, 800) - mean per patch
    
    # Compute motion weights per patch
    motion_weights = F.avg_pool2d(motion_grad, patch_size)  # (1, 1, 20, 40)
    motion_weights = motion_weights.view(1, -1)  # (1, 800)
    motion_weights = motion_weights / (motion_weights.sum() + 1e-8)  # Normalize
    
    # Weighted score
    weighted_score = (diff_per_patch * motion_weights).sum()
    
    return weighted_score.item()

# Usage:
weighted_score_normal = compute_motion_weighted_score(
    pred_stud_normal, pred_teacher_normal, data_normal['motion_grad_avg'])
weighted_score_anomaly = compute_motion_weighted_score(
    pred_stud_anomaly, pred_teacher_anomaly, data_anomaly['motion_grad_avg'])

print(f"Motion-weighted score (Normal): {weighted_score_normal:.4f}")
print(f"Motion-weighted score (Anomaly): {weighted_score_anomaly:.4f}")

Why This Might Work Better:¶

Method Normal Frame Anomaly Frame
Paper's mean Low (background dilutes) Slightly higher (still diluted)
My motion-weighted Focus on person walking (normal motion) Focus on bicycle (abnormal motion!)

I Just Found a Research Direction! 🚀¶

This could be:

  • A simple improvement to the baseline
  • Or an ablation study showing motion-weighted scoring improves detection

Freeman would say: "You've built intuition about WHY something works (or doesn't), and proposed a hypothesis to test!"

Want to implement this and compare scores?
